message: Jake
Yuriko
Boss, thank you for your hard work. Meryl must have had a tough time, right? But the world is getting terrible, isn't it?
The idea that Guy is the culprit...
That's outrageous, isn't it?
Absolutely. How did we end up with that story?
Well, just saying someone is missing at this time only adds to the suspicion. Plus, someone involved is dead.
What? So you think Guy is the culprit?
No, that's not it. I'm just saying that if you think generally, it tends to lead to that assumption.
Oh, welcome back.
Thank you for your hard work, Boss.

With me
it feels like it has been a while since there's been such a calm atmosphere.
There's no one likely to stop by, and the phone has been silent. I feel like the only place I can settle down is this office.
What I can hear is
someone's voice and the faint sound of the air conditioning. Outside, it’s quiet as if the noise is a lie.
Always looking lively and unchanged. This kid seems to have no moods or bad days.
When Meryl met Cindy, she really made a huge blunder,
but she doesn’t seem affected at all.
In a mixed crowd like New York, Oriental people are not unusual.
Boss, please don't glare at me with that scary face.
I don't think Guy killed Cindy.
Well, sure, if you think about it normally, he might seem suspicious.
See? You think that too, right, Boss? It's mostly Guy's fault. He doesn't show up even when things go this way.
Come on, come on.
Don't blame him too much. Guy probably has his reasons for not showing up.
But! Because of that, Kristy ended up in that situation!
That's true. When it comes to Kristy's issue, Guy is fully responsible.
See? Just like I said, right? Boss, please find Guy and deal with him.
That's right.
Now, stop slacking off.
Time to get to work!
Was Meryl pretty rough?
Yeah.
It was really tough. I understand how you feel, but demanding they hand over Guy was intense.
So you’re really buying into the theory that Guy is the culprit, huh, her?
At this point, without him around, it’s unlikely that Meryl would commit a crime, but if Guy comes back...
Ah, it’s a situation where you can’t just be happy.
Everything’s just a total mess.
How did it end up like this?
Geez, that idiot of a criminal!
I feel so sorry for Meryl; I don't know how she can face anyone.
Women really are such mysterious creatures, aren’t they?
Huh?
What did you suddenly say? What’s going on with you?
Well, it was just such a shocking fact.
Did something happen with Kristy?
N-No.
It’s not like that.
Something feels off.
Boss.
You’ve got something to feel guilty about, don’t you?
Kristy can’t be feeling at peace either. Her beloved is being called a murderer.
That must be taking a toll on her mentally.
She’s definitely dealing with it.
Given what we know about Kristy, considering past lessons, she’s in a really tough situation.
No matter how rich she is, I feel sorry for Kristy. If I say that, I might get in trouble, though.
Cindy was found in the trash; apart from having her left leg and right ankle severed, she was still beautiful.
But if she’s dead, what can we do about it?
That’s such a rude way to say it. Cindy didn’t want to die, you know. At least she died beautiful.
But you were discussing something unpleasant, right? You said she had her legs cut off while she was still alive.
This criminal is already not human anymore. They have completely lost all human emotions like pain.
That might be true. But in reality, they could just be an ordinary person found anywhere.
Cindy must be feeling sad.
I wonder if Gloria is doing okay?
There's no way she could be okay after losing Cindy.
That's right. What will she do from now on?
I wonder how she'll live from now on.
Although it was Gloria who received the compensation from the Nets Office, why do you think she allowed gossip about Cindy?
Well...
It's strange, considering how that person seemed to risk everything to protect Cindy.
It is strange, isn't it? It's hard to believe Gloria would do anything negative towards Cindy.
Since then, I haven't heard from Tim. He must be going through a tough time too, but I hope he can get through it.
He'll be fine.
That's true. Being young, it will probably soon become just a memory for him.
Tim will be fine, he's still young.
Cindy was killed by Tony, right?
You're saying that again. Do you really want to believe Tony is the criminal?
He's a suspicious person.
But I think he's different.
But that was said by Tony's boyfriend, who's also a man. I still don't want to give up on the theory that Tony is the criminal.
There's no evidence that can definitively label him as the criminal.
But there is a bloody scalpel, isn't there? How do you explain that?
Well, that's still...
One day, we'll catch him red-handed.
I think it's really suspicious.
Guy, if he doesn't come back like this, it's going to be a big problem, right?
Yeah.
I can't say it's in good condition.
I don't care what happens to that heartless guy, but I’m worried about the Boss and Kristy.
I think it's better for him to come out quickly.
I wonder if he even knows there’s such news being reported.
There’s a possibility of that, I guess.
Ahh, what kind of countryside is he hiding in?!
Let’s find him quickly and give him a good scolding!
It seems like they’re getting along lately, but sadly, there hasn’t been any calls.
Do you have some complaints or what?
Not really.
Yes, indeed. Hohoho.
I have no intention of meddling in the Boss’s private life. If you could just solve the serial murder case like a detective, I wouldn’t complain.
That’s harsh.
If you don’t believe you can do it, you’ll be a lousy detective forever.
Yo!
If you’re that concerned, why not just go meet him?
I heard from Gloria that she supposedly received some compensation from that BAM article.
Gloria did?
It's more accurate to say it's from Cindy's side. Seems like she received it in advance from the Nets Office.
Cindy didn’t say anything like that, right?
Cindy probably wasn’t informed. I think she's not the type to let that slide.
Ah, that’s true. But what's up with Nets?
That gossip—was it set up by that Garrison?
We haven't confirmed that yet.
What’s the benefit of doing something like that?
I wonder what the objective was.
Meryl misunderstands Guy as her daughter's enemy. If she meets Guy now, she'll definitely try to kill him.
How can I convince her that Guy isn't the culprit?
It might be necessary to catch the real culprit.
For now, it's better not to take my eyes off Meryl. If I’m not careful, she might become a murderer.
Kristy
She mistook me for Guy. In her desperation to find him, she behaved like that.
But seeing my old lover like that is just so uncomfortable.
I don't think she'll just do it to anyone.
Really, what is going to happen to Kristy? I'm very worried.
She's already not feeling well.
I wonder what will happen if she sees the matter concerning Guy.
Anyway, I won't be able to do anything until I find Guy and take him to Kristy.
I also can't take my eyes off Kristy.
Cindy's death has been reported, and the public connects it to a serial murder.
But is that really correct?
It's possible to think that the culprit who killed Cindy is just mimicking the methods of the serial murder case.
With such good timing, I even feel like Cindy's murder was caused by the mother-daughter meeting.
She must have known it would negatively affect Cindy, so why did she accept Nets' offer?
I just can't figure it out.
Gloria cared for Cindy so much.
She was in quite a shock after losing Cindy. It would be pretty hard for her to recover.
Tim was the closest to Cindy; I wonder if he knows why Cindy had to be killed.
I can't imagine Tim being able to keep a secret, and he must have told me everything he knows.
I hope he forgets about Cindy soon and gets used to his new life.
Tony just feels way too suspicious.
If he's the serial killer, it fits a bit too well.
Given how shady he is, it might be easy for someone to frame Tony as the criminal.
So Tony was bisexual, huh? Drugs and womanizing.
And even messing around with guys.
What a wide-ranging guy.
With reporting like that, it makes it sound like Guy is definitely the culprit.
Now that we're in this situation, if he's alive somewhere, shouldn't he come out?
If he doesn't show up soon, he might really be taken for the criminal.
Jennifer was close with Cindy, so she must be pretty shocked.
It's unfortunate, but I can't just focus on Jennifer.
The priority is to resolve the case.
I wonder what he's doing right now.
Behind Nets leaking Guy's gossip, there must be some issue with contract negotiations.
But why resort to such measures?
I think there were better ways to handle things, especially since they were under house arrest.
It seems like Garrison still has something he's hiding.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
